key_assumptions.input: Starting free cash flow per share
key_assumptions.source: FY2025 adjusted after-tax income $4044.0M ÷ 570.35M shares = $7.09/sh; excludes investment marks, Fortitude noise, and Corebridge fair-value swings
Combined ratio near 90%, investment yields hold, repurchases continue; 10× exit on year-10 owner cash

open_questions: Normalized owner cash uses adjusted after-tax income, not GAAP EPS
Global Personal wildfire exposure and Fortitude runoff remain judgment bands
No approved third-party sources in base IRR
thesis: American International Group, Inc. (NYSE: **AIG**) is a global property-casualty insurer focused on General Insurance after the 2022 Corebridge Financial separation. **[Fact]** FY2025 net income attributable to AIG common shareholders was **$3.1 billion** and book value per share was **$76.44** (adjusted book value **$78.02** excluding Investments AOCI). **[Fact]** Adjusted after-tax income, the company's normalized earnings measure excluding investment marks, Fortitude Re noise, and Corebridge fair-value swings, was **$4.0 billion**, or about **$7.09 per diluted share** on **570.4 million** weighted average diluted shares. **[Fact]** General Insurance delivered **$2.3 billion** of underwriting income in FY2025 (combined ratio **90.1%**), supplemented by **$3.4 billion** of segment net investment income. **[Inference]** AIG is a post-turnaround compounder: it underwrites commercial and personal risk where multinational expertise creates pricing power, invests float at higher yields, and returns excess capital through aggressive repurchases (shares outstanding fell from **688.8M** in 2023 to **538.2M** at December 31, 2025).
why_market_wrong: There is **no classic dated mispricing signal** (HK predictive attribute: **none**). **[Inference]** Generalists may anchor on GAAP net income volatility from realized investment gains and Corebridge mark-to-market moves, understating repeatable owner cash versus adjusted after-tax income of **$7.09 per share**. **[Fact]** The stock trades near **1.0 times** adjusted book value (**$78.02**), embedding continued combined ratio discipline and buybacks. **[Fact]** North America Commercial and International Commercial combined ratios were **86.8%** and **86.9%** in FY2025, while Global Personal was **99.0%** near breakeven. **[Inference]** The market may underweight the capital-return engine (share count down **22%** in two years) or overweight catastrophe tail risk without recognizing the **$920M** FY2025 cat charges already absorbed in results. Return here is **normalized owner cash at price**, not a simple price-to-book trade.
